Off Calendar Jun-07-2023 Continued To Aug-02-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Enoch Wang, Esq. 415-837-3101 Fife Law Group for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Lily Duong, Esq. 415-576-3000 Baker McKenzie LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms Dong contests the tenative ruling and presents arguments. Mr. Wang responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANAPLAN, INC.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

The FAC adequately pleads a breach of contract claim. (Id. at 4:27-5:14.) It asserts that defendant breached a termination agreement "by failing to provide" him "with adequate information and instructions to exercise his option rights." (Id. at 5:8-10.) Defendant's arguments to the contrary rely on its spin of documents of which it seeks judicial notice. However, defendant "disputes the authenticity and applicability of the unsworn documents" (Opp. 1:28.) and their contents are not subject to judicial notice in any event. (Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114-15 ["court ruling on a demurrer cannot take judicial notice of the proper interpretation of a document submitted in support of the demurrer"]; see also Evid. Code 451-52.)

The FAC adequately pleads a claim for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing that is not wholly duplicative of its breach of contract claim. (Id. at 5:16-6:14.)

The FAC adequately asserts declaratory relief, which can be pled in the alternative to other counts. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B, Uler Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
